title: Vocational Viability
question: Does this work still feel like work I want to do?
whatsUnderThreat: The intrinsic reward of practicing the craft as its texture shifts from sustained problem-solving to orchestration and review. The dimension most likely to be dismissed as soft, and most likely to predict who quietly leaves the field over the next decade.

reading.text: Journal protocol still in legal review; separate consent instrument required before first reading. Auto-derived hours-on-craft-outside-paid signal shows a 3-week dip mid-quarter, mostly absorbed back.

consentNote: Separate, granular consent required. Not all mentees opt in — appropriate, given the personal nature of the data.

measurements.question: What am I noting, week by week, about the texture of the work itself?
measurements.method: Agent walks through a structured journal at end of week. Free-form responses get coded for sentiment and theme by an AI analyzer.
measurements.note: Protocol in legal review. First reading awaits separate cohort consent instrument.

measurements.question: Does each session leave me energized, or depleted?
measurements.method: Agent posts a 1–7 rating prompt at session close.

measurements.question: How much time am I spending on the craft outside paid hours?
measurements.method: Calendar + transcript timestamps, with each session tagged client/paid vs. personal based on which repo or project is active.

series.label: Hours per week on personal craft

measurements.question: Would I want to keep practicing exactly this way for another ten years?
measurements.method: Annual long-form reflection. Coded yes / no / uncertain summary marker on the timeline.
